name: B.S. in Civil Engineering

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The eight parsed semesters (requirementGroups 8) give strong coverage of all major civil subdisciplines in a sensible order: a math/science/computing/CAD foundation (MATH 111/112, CHEM 125/126, PHYS 111, CS 101, CE 101 CAD), the engineering-mechanics spine (MECH 235 Statics, MECH 239 Strength of Materials, MECH 236 Dynamics, CE 332 Structural Analysis), then subdiscipline courses fanning out into structures (CE 333 Reinforced Concrete, CE 432 Steel), geotechnical (CE 341 Soil Mechanics, CE 443 Foundation Design), water (CE 320 Fluid Mechanics, CE 321 Water Resources), transportation (CE 350), materials (CE 210, CE 360 Sustainable Materials) and environmental (ENE 262), closing with CE 494/495 Design I/II. Coherence is very good; it is held just below exemplary because the fourth-year first semester embeds an inline GER/COM selection list that muddies the otherwise clean term structure.
dimensions.suggestion: Pull the embedded '300-level GER: select one of the following (COM 312/316/319/337/339)' block out of the Year 4 term grid into a separate 'GER options' callout so the capstone term reads cleanly.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: The prerequisite graph has reasonable depth for a broad civil program (deepestPrereqChain 8) and follows the expected mechanics-to-design flow (Statics into Strength of Materials into Structural Analysis into Concrete/Steel design; Fluid Mechanics into Water Resources). missingCourseCount is 10, but the missingCoursesSample (CE 101, CE 200A, CE 320A, CE 341A, CE 431, CHEM 125A, GER 200, GER 300, PHYS 111A, PHYS 121A) is overwhelmingly co-scheduled labs and GER placeholders rather than substantive broken links, so the working graph is sound.
dimensions.suggestion: Reconcile the co-listed CE lab sections (CE 200A, CE 320A, CE 341A, CE 431) and the science labs (CHEM 125A, PHYS 111A/121A) with the catalog so the ten dangling references drop toward zero.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or educational objectives appear in the captured data; the page provides the plan of study and course descriptions only. For an ABET civil program this is a meaningful omission because accreditation and licensure expectations are framed in terms of explicit student outcomes not shown here.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish 4-6 program outcomes covering design across civil subdisciplines, professional/ethical practice, and the CE 494/495 capstone competencies, and note the path toward FE/PE licensure.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: The plan reconciles exactly (summedTermCredits 120, statedTotalCredits 120, creditReconcileDelta 0) and is well balanced: a broad required core spanning every civil subdiscipline, math/basic science, humanities/GER and HSS, plus three genuine elective slots (CE Elective, CE Designated Elective, Science Elective) and IE 492 Engineering Management. Breadth and depth are both accommodated within the 120-credit envelope.
dimensions.suggestion: Add per-line credit values to the null-credit GER/HSS rows so each term's contribution to the 120 total is explicit.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The page is largely clear and complete, with eight labeled terms, per-term subtotals, and full course titles. Readability is reduced by the Year 4 first-semester term containing an inline multi-option COM/GER selection list (COM 339/312/319/337/316 all shown with null credits), which makes that term look overloaded and ambiguous, and by several null-coded GER placeholder rows.
dimensions.suggestion: Render selection-based requirements ('choose one of...') as a single labeled line with a credit value rather than listing every option inside the term grid.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The curriculum is solidly current across the traditional civil subdisciplines and includes a modern note in CE 360 Sustainable Civil Engineering Materials and an environmental-engineering touchpoint (ENE 262). The main currency gaps are digital-delivery tools (BIM/GIS beyond the basic CE 101 CAD), infrastructure resilience/climate adaptation, and smart-infrastructure sensing, all of which are increasingly expected of new civil graduates.
dimensions.suggestion: Add or embed a BIM/GIS digital-delivery component and an infrastructure-resilience/climate-adaptation module (e.g., extending CE 321 Water Resources) to modernize the design sequence.

descriptionQuality.justification: All 32 sampled courses carry present, specific descriptions with none thin, and titles clearly convey scope across the subdisciplines (CE 332 Structural Analysis, CE 341 Soil Mechanics, CE 320 Fluid Mechanics, CE 360 Sustainable Civil Engineering Materials, CE 494/495 Design). Only the excerpt text is truncated in the capture.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisites follow the expected mechanics-and-design ordering with grade conditions on the core chain and clear course-to-course dependencies. The deduction reflects the ten dangling references, which although mostly co-listed labs (CE 200A, CE 320A, CE 341A, CE 431) and GER placeholders, still leave several catalog links unresolved.

missingTopics.topic: Building Information Modeling (BIM) and digital project delivery
missingTopics.rationale: CE 101 provides only basic CAD; the program has no BIM/parametric-modeling course despite BIM being a baseline expectation across structural, construction and infrastructure practice.
CE 200 Surveying is traditional; adding GIS would connect surveying, water-resources (CE 321) and transportation (CE 350) planning to the geospatial workflows employers now assume.
CE 321 Water Resources and the structural sequence provide the technical base, but no course addresses flood/coastal resilience, extreme-load design or climate-adaptation planning.

missingTopics.topic: Structural health monitoring and smart sensing
missingTopics.rationale: With CE 332/333/432 covering analysis and design, there is no exposure to instrumentation, sensor networks or data-based condition assessment of infrastructure.
CE 360 introduces sustainable materials, but the program lacks a systematic LCA/embodied-carbon method course to quantify sustainability across designs.

emergingTrends.rationale: Extending CE 321 Water Resources and the structural design courses toward resilience and extreme-event design matches the dominant near-term direction of civil practice and funding.

emergingTrends.trend: Digital twins, BIM and AI in construction management
emergingTrends.rationale: Coupling IE 492 and the CE 494/495 capstone with BIM and data-driven construction analytics reflects rapid digitalization of project delivery.
CE 360 is a strong anchor to expand into low-carbon concrete, recycled aggregates and embodied-carbon design as decarbonization reaches the built environment.
Sensor-instrumented bridges and pavements are becoming standard; a monitoring/IoT component would modernize the structural and transportation tracks.

emergingTrends.trend: Green stormwater infrastructure and nature-based solutions
emergingTrends.rationale: Linking CE 321 and ENE 262, green-infrastructure design responds to the shift in water management toward nature-based, distributed solutions.

topSuggestions: Publish explicit program-level learning outcomes / educational objectives (currently absent), aligned to the CE 494/495 capstone and to FE/PE licensure expectations.
Add digital-delivery competency (BIM and GIS) beyond the basic CE 101 CAD to meet a now-standard employer expectation.
Introduce infrastructure-resilience / climate-adaptation content, most naturally by extending CE 321 Water Resources and the structural design sequence.
Clean up the Year 4 term grid by moving the inline COM/GER 'choose one' selection block into a separate labeled options callout with a credit value.
Reconcile the ten dangling references (co-listed CE and science labs plus GER placeholders) so the prerequisite graph is fully connected.
